L1.area: Groovy script compilation and code transformation pipeline

L2.description: A sandbox bypass vulnerability exists where default parameter expressions in class constructors are not properly intercepted by the security sandbox during code transformation, allowing execution of arbitrary unsandboxed code when a Groovy script is evaluated.

L3.area: Sandbox code transformation and expression interception logic for class constructor handling
L3.description: A sandbox bypass vulnerability exists where default parameter expressions in class constructors that delegate to a superclass constructor via super() are not transformed by the security sandbox, allowing execution of arbitrary unsandboxed code. The issue occurs because initial expressions for constructor parameters are not processed when the constructor is handled separately due to super-constructor delegation.

ground_truth.reason: Default parameter expressions in constructors that call super() bypass the Groovy sandbox transformation, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary unsandboxed code such as calling restricted APIs.
